text.source_text: ज्ञातु मि च्छा जिज्ञासा । अवगति पर्यन्तं ज्ञानं सन्वाच्याया इच्छायाः कर्म , फल विषयत्वादिच्छायाः । ज्ञानेन हि प्रमाणेनावगन्तुमिष्टं ब्रह्म । ब्रह्मावगतिर्हि पुरुषार्थः , निःशेष संसारबीजाविद्याद्यनर्थनिबर्हणात् । तस्मा द्ब्र ह्म जिज्ञासितव्यम् ॥
text.transliteration_iast: jñātu mi cchā jijñāsā | avagati paryantaṃ jñānaṃ sanvācyāyā icchāyāḥ karma , phala viṣayatvādicchāyāḥ | jñānena hi pramāṇenāvagantumiṣṭaṃ brahma | brahmāvagatirhi puruṣārthaḥ , niḥśeṣa saṃsārabījāvidyādyanarthanibarhaṇāt | tasmā dbra hma jijñāsitavyam ||

translation.literal_translation: The desire to know is jijnasa. Knowledge culminating in ascertainment is the object of that verbalized desire, since desire has result as its object. For Brahman is what is desired to be known through the pramana of knowledge. Realization of Brahman is indeed the highest human end, because it removes the entire complex of evils beginning with ignorance, the seed of samsara. Therefore Brahman must be inquired into.
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya defines jijnasa as iccha directed toward avagati and ties brahma-avagati to purushartha via complete avidya-nivritti.
translation.interpretive_note: Defines inquiry as purposive cognition aimed at liberation, not curiosity.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Defined as an intentional orientation terminating in avagati (ascertaining knowledge).
Serves as proper fulfillment of inquiry rather than mere curiosity.

lexical_notes.gloss: relation of desire to knowledge as its object
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Clarifies semantic structure of jijnasa expression.
Teleological justification for Brahma-jijnasa in the sutra opening.
Explains why Brahman-knowledge alone removes samsara root.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S01 pass with stable inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit janmadi-lakshana and sastra-yonitva technical compression in records 1.1.008-1.1.015.
